name: Resend
tagline: Send email from any agent with one API key.
description: Resend is an email API built for developers. The official MCP server exposes sending, contacts, audiences, broadcasts, and domain management; the REST API is small enough for an agent to learn in one read.

methods.label: API key
methods.note: One key, sent as a bearer header. Scoped keys can restrict to sending-only.

auth.guide:
Create an API key at [resend.com/api-keys](https://resend.com/api-keys) (`re_…`). Keys can be **full access** or restricted to **sending access** only — prefer the latter for agents that just send mail.

```bash
curl https://api.resend.com/emails \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $RESEND_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"from": "you@yourdomain.com", "to": ["them@example.com"], "subject": "Hi", "text": "Hello from an agent."}'
```

**MCP (stdio):** the official server runs locally with the same key:

```bash
claude mcp add resend -e RESEND_API_KEY=re_xxxxxxxxx -- npx -y resend-mcp
```

You'll need a verified sending domain before production sends — set one up under [Domains](https://resend.com/domains).

interfaces.name: Resend MCP
interfaces.install: claude mcp add resend -e RESEND_API_KEY=re_xxx -- npx -y resend-mcp
interfaces.note: Runs locally over stdio with your API key.
